failure_surface: The agent's solution using COUNTA with mixed references failed validation, suggesting the formula logic did not correctly produce sequential numbering based on column B's data presence.
root_cause: The agent applied COUNTA(B$2:B{row}) formulas without verifying the actual data structure and content in the target worksheet before implementation. The formula assumes column B contains the trigger data, but the agent did not programmatically confirm this assumption against the actual spreadsheet state.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before writing formulas, always programmatically inspect the actual column headers and data layout in the relevant worksheet to confirm assumptions about which column contains which data type.
evidence: The spreadsheet_content showed ambiguous column alignment ('#     A') that was not programmatically verified before applying formulas
The agent wrote formulas directly without first reading existing cell values or confirming column B actually contained the referenced data
No sheet inspection occurred despite SKILL.md recommendation to 'Always list available sheet names programmatically (wb.sheetnames) before attempting access'
The formula strategy assumed column B was the trigger column without validating the actual data structure

memory_items.title: Verify Data Layout Before Formula Writing
memory_items.lesson: When creating formulas that reference specific columns, programmatically read the worksheet structure to confirm column positions and data types before implementation.
memory_items.generalization: Always read and validate the actual spreadsheet structure using openpyxl or pandas inspection methods before applying cell-level formulas that depend on specific column locations.

memory_items.title: Confirm Sheet Context for Formulas
memory_items.lesson: Column-level operations assume knowledge of which sheet contains the target data; always verify the active worksheet and its available sheet names first.
memory_items.generalization: Use wb.sheetnames and ws.sheet_name checks at the start of any formula-writing operation to ensure formulas target the correct worksheet context.

memory_items.title: Validate Cell Contents Before Assumption-Based Logic
memory_items.lesson: Formulas that depend on conditions (like counting non-empty cells) require verification of actual cell contents before determining the formula pattern.
memory_items.generalization: Read a sample of cells in the target range using openpyxl's iteration methods to confirm data types and patterns before designing dependent formula logic.

edits.content:
## 6. Pre-Implementation Verification
- **Data Structure Check**: Before writing formulas that depend on specific column data, programmatically read cell values to verify the expected data layout matches assumptions.
- **Worksheet Confirmation**: Always verify the active worksheet name and available sheets match the task scope before applying cell modifications.
- **Sample Validation**: Read a sample of cells in the target range using ws.iter_rows() or ws.cell() to confirm data types and patterns before implementing dependent formula logic.
